papers.title: Monthly HIV-drug injections offer potent alternative to daily tablets
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Researchers have developed a long-lasting HIV treatment that can be administered through monthly injections, providing an alternative to daily tablets for people with mental illness or other conditions that make it hard to adhere to standard treatments.

papers.title: Briefing chat: What Galileo’s scribbled margin notes reveal about his scientific journey
papers.authors: Benjamin Thompson, Maren Hunsberger
papers.summary: A recent Nature briefing chat discusses Galileo's scientific journey based on his margin notes, revealing insights into his thought process and contributions to science.

papers.title: Electric-vehicle batteries toughen up to beat the heat
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Electric-vehicle batteries are improving to withstand rising temperatures, which can shorten battery life, and are expected to keep up with the pace of climate change.

papers.title: Peanut-processing microbes ward off dangerous allergic shock
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Scientists have identified bacteria that can digest allergy-triggering compounds in peanuts, potentially leading to a new treatment for peanut allergies.

papers.title: Modelling the cosmos and imagining a future without meat: Books in brief
papers.authors: Andrew Robinson
papers.summary: Andrew Robinson reviews five science books, including one about modelling the cosmos and another about a future without meat.

papers.title: The missing pieces of menopause science
papers.authors: Linda Nordling
papers.summary: A recent article discusses the return of hormone therapy for treating perimenopause, but notes that there are still gaps in the evidence for its effectiveness.

papers.title: What a viral TikTok taught me about personal storytelling in science
papers.authors: Jiun Youn
papers.summary: A researcher reflects on the power of personal storytelling in science, inspired by a viral TikTok video that showcased a student's educational video.

papers.title: Celebrate women in research and the networks that sustain them
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A Nature article celebrates women in research and the networks that support them, highlighting the importance of mentorship on International Women's Day.

papers.title: Climate change is speeding up — the pace nearly doubled in ten years
papers.authors: Alexandra Witze
papers.summary: A recent analysis finds that the pace of climate change has nearly doubled in the past decade, with the Earth warming at a rate of around 0.35 ºC per decade.

papers.title: Genetically modified pig liver keeps man alive until human organ transplant
papers.authors: Rachel Fieldhouse
papers.summary: A genetically modified pig liver was used to keep a man alive until a human organ transplant could be performed, marking the first time this procedure has been done on a living person.

papers.title: Uncharted: Understanding women’s health across the body
papers.authors: Jyoti Madhusoodanan
papers.summary: A study highlights the importance of disaggregating data by sex to improve diagnostics and treatments for women.

papers.title: Cracked, but still there: the glass ceiling persists for senior women in science
papers.authors: Mangala Srinivas
papers.summary: A commentary reminds junior colleagues that career success does not protect against gender-based bias in science.

papers.title: Author Correction: Programmable 200 GOPS Hopfield-inspired photonic Ising machine
papers.authors: Nayem Al-Kayed, Charles St-Arnault, Hugh Morison, A. Aadhi, Chaoran Huang et al. (+3 more)
papers.summary: An author correction is made for a previous study on a Hopfield-inspired photonic Ising machine.

papers.title: The first ‘AI societies’ are taking shape: how human-like are they?
papers.authors: Jenna Ahart
papers.summary: Scientists are studying 'social' interactions between artificial-intelligence agents to understand their human-like behavior.

papers.title: These brain cells clear proteins that contribute to Alzheimer’s
papers.authors: Rachel Fieldhouse
papers.summary: Specialized brain cells called tanycytes help clear proteins that contribute to Alzheimer's disease.

papers.title: Daily briefing: How DNA testing can tell identical twins apart
papers.authors: Flora Graham
papers.summary: A new forensic method can differentiate between identical twins in criminal cases.

papers.title: The Epstein scandal is a wake-up call — new rules are needed on links with rich donors
papers.authors: Arthur Caplan
papers.summary: A systemic failure of oversight allowed Jeffrey Epstein to curry favor with academia, highlighting the need for stronger governance.

papers.title: Identical twins on trial: can DNA testing tell them apart?
papers.authors: Rachel Fieldhouse
papers.summary: Emerging scientific methods can help distinguish between identical twins in criminal cases, where conventional DNA analysis may fail.

papers.title: Inside Mexico’s stem-cell industry
papers.authors: Gemma Conroy
papers.summary: Researchers in Mexico are calling for stronger regulation of stem-cell treatments, which many people travel long distances to receive.

papers.title: How AI is shaping the war in Iran — and what’s next for future conflicts
papers.authors: Nicola Jones
papers.summary: The conflict in Iran is highlighting the use of artificial intelligence in warfare, with implications for future conflicts.

papers.title: Do obesity drugs treat addiction? Huge study hints at their promise
papers.authors: Mariana Lenharo
papers.summary: A large study of 600,000 US military veterans found that those who took anti-obesity medications were less likely to develop complications related to substance-use disorders.

papers.title: First ‘half Möbius’ carbon chain wows chemists
papers.authors: Davide Castelvecchi
papers.summary: Scientists have created a unique 'half Möbius' carbon chain molecule with an unusual looped shape, which could unlock new physical properties.

papers.title: High-rise transistors can be used to build space-saving circuits
papers.authors: Xiong Xiong, Yanqing Wu
papers.summary: Researchers have developed a new method to build compact and fast electronic devices using high-rise transistors made from nanosheet stacks.

papers.title: How these koalas bounced back from the brink of extinction
papers.authors: Mohana Basu
papers.summary: A study of koalas that were on the brink of extinction found that tracking DNA recombination offers hope for other species that have lost genetic diversity.

papers.title: Women in science are not a ‘problem to be fixed’
papers.authors: Holly Newson
papers.summary: A book review challenges two false visions of gender equality at work and offers a fairer future for the female workforce.

papers.title: Shared neural substrates of prosocial and parenting behaviours
papers.authors: Fangmiao Sun, Kayla Y. Lim, James Dang, Li I. Zhang, Ye Emily Wu et al. (+1 more)
papers.summary: This study identified shared neural substrates in the brain associated with prosocial and parenting behaviors. These findings suggest a common neural basis for behaviors that promote social bonding and care for others. The study's results have implications for understanding the neural mechanisms underlying social behavior and may inform the development of interventions for social and emotional development.

papers.title: Heart surgery with quick-setting magnetic fluid could prevent strokes
papers.authors: Nick Petrić Howe, Shamini Bundell
papers.summary: Researchers have developed a quick-setting magnetic fluid that can be injected into the heart to prevent strokes. The fluid safely fills the area where blood clots can form, and animal trials have shown strong evidence of its effectiveness. This breakthrough has the potential to revolutionize stroke prevention and treatment.

papers.title: Magnetic gel injected into the heart could stop strokes
papers.authors: Nick Petrić Howe
papers.summary: A new magnetic gel has been developed that can be injected into the heart to stop strokes. The gel-based plug blocks a part of the heart, reducing the risk of stroke, and has been shown to be effective in animal trials. This innovation has the potential to save lives and improve stroke treatment outcomes.

papers.title: Genome modelling and design across all domains of life with Evo 2
papers.authors: Garyk Brixi, Matthew G. Durrant, Jerome Ku, Mohsen Naghipourfar, Michael Poli et al. (+57 more)
papers.summary: Evo 2 is a new artificial intelligence-based biological foundation model that predicts functional properties from genomic sequences and provides a rich generative model for researchers in biology. The model was trained on 9 trillion DNA base pairs spanning all domains of life and has the potential to revolutionize genome modeling and design.

papers.title: DICER cleavage fidelity is governed by 5′-end binding pockets
papers.authors: Minh Khoa Ngo, Cong Truc Le, Tuan Anh Nguyen
papers.summary: Researchers have shed light on the mechanism by which DICER, a key enzyme in the RNase III family, cleaves RNA at precise locations to produce small RNAs. Cryo-electron microscopy and massively parallel assays revealed that DICER's 5'-end binding pockets play a crucial role in its cleavage fidelity.

papers.title: Limited thermal tolerance in tropical insects and its genomic signature
papers.authors: Kim L. Holzmann, Thomas Schmitzer, Antonia Abels, Marko Čorkalo, Oliver Mitesser et al. (+13 more)
papers.summary: A study of tropical insects found that species from lowland areas have low thermal tolerance and that this is due to the fundamental properties of protein architecture.

papers.title: A metabolic alarmin from keratinocytes potentiates systemic humoral immunity
papers.authors: Zhenglin Ji, Ji Gao, Shaocun Zhang, Jiajie Li, Haijing Wu et al. (+39 more)
papers.summary: A metabolic intermediate called farnesyl pyrophosphate acts as an alarmin to enhance the immune system's antibody responses.

papers.title: Long-term thrombus-free left atrial appendage occlusion via magnetofluids
papers.authors: Shu Wang, Wenhao Ju, Donglin Zhuang, Zhecheng Chen, Dongliang Zhao et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: Researchers developed a method using magnetofluids to occlude the left atrial appendage without forming a thrombus, offering a personalized strategy.

papers.title: Lipid metabolism drives dietary effects on T cell ferroptosis and immunity
papers.authors: Naiqi Wang, Zhian Chen, Yin Yao, Chenglong Sun, Wei Wei et al. (+35 more)
papers.summary: The balance of fatty acids in the diet affects the susceptibility of T cells to ferroptosis and their function in the immune system.

papers.title: Merlin: a computed tomography vision–language foundation model and dataset
papers.authors: Louis Blankemeier, Ashwin Kumar, Joseph Paul Cohen, Jiaming Liu, Longchao Liu et al. (+35 more)
papers.summary: A new vision-language foundation model called Merlin was developed and trained on a large dataset of CT scans and patient records, demonstrating strong performance across various tasks.

papers.title: Microbiota-mediated induction of beige adipocytes in response to dietary cues
papers.authors: Takeshi Tanoue, Manabu Nagayama, Ayumi J. A. Roochana, Samuel Zimmerman, Orr Ashenberg et al. (+28 more)
papers.summary: A low-protein diet in mice leads to a change in their fat tissue, with the help of their gut bacteria, to burn more energy like brown fat.

papers.title: Mechanism of co-transcriptional cap snatching by influenza polymerase
papers.authors: Alexander Helmut Rotsch, Delong Li, Maud Dupont, Tim Krischuns, Ute Neef et al. (+9 more)
papers.summary: Researchers have discovered how the influenza virus uses the host cell's machinery to make copies of its genetic material.

papers.title: Cell-free chromatin state tracing reveals disease origin and therapy responses
papers.authors: Xubin Chen, Xiaoxuan Meng, Weilong Zhang, Xiawei Zhang, Yaping Zhang et al. (+26 more)
papers.summary: Scientists have developed a new method to analyze DNA in the blood to identify the source of diseases and track their progression.

papers.title: The oldest articulated bony fish from the early Silurian period
papers.authors: You-An Zhu, Yang Chen, Qiang Li, Wen-Jin Zhao, Zheng-Da Zhou et al. (+7 more)
papers.summary: Fossil evidence has revealed the oldest known remains of a bony fish, dating back to the early Silurian period.

papers.title: Bulk hexagonal diamond
papers.authors: Shoulong Lai, Xigui Yang, Jiuyang Shi, Shijie Liu, Ying Guo et al. (+7 more)
papers.summary: Researchers have successfully synthesized millimeter-sized hexagonal diamond, a new form of diamond, by compressing graphite under high pressure and temperature.

papers.title: Sea level much higher than assumed in most coastal hazard assessments
papers.authors: Katharina Seeger, Philip S. J. Minderhoud
papers.summary: A meta-analysis of global coastal mean sea level measurements reveals that the actual level is higher than previously assumed in most coastal hazard assessments.

papers.title: A glucocorticoid–FAS axis controls immune evasion during metastatic seeding
papers.authors: Monica Cassandras, Xavier Sanchez, Lauren Hsu, Yu Huang, Adam J. Getzler et al. (+17 more)
papers.summary: Glucocorticoid receptor activation is a key driver of resistance to immune cells in triple-negative breast cancer during initial metastatic seeding.

papers.title: The molecular basis of force selectivity by PIEZO2
papers.authors: Eric M. Mulhall, Oleg Yarishkin, Rose Z. Hill, Anna K. Koster, Ardem Patapoutian
papers.summary: Research reveals the molecular basis of force selectivity by the PIEZO2 channel, which is intrinsically more rigid than PIEZO1.

papers.title: Advancing operational global aerosol forecasting with machine learning
papers.authors: Ke Gui, Xutao Zhang, Huizheng Che, Lei Li, Yu Zheng et al. (+17 more)
papers.summary: A machine-learning-driven forecasting system achieves reliable 5-day, 3-hourly forecasts of aerosol optical components and surface concentrations.

papers.title: Wind shear enhances soil moisture influence on rapid thunderstorm growth
papers.authors: Christopher M. Taylor, Cornelia Klein, Emma J. Barton, Sebastian Hahn, Wolfgang Wagner
papers.summary: Analysis of millions of events shows that wind shear amplifies the impact of soil moisture in triggering rapidly developing thunderstorms.

papers.title: Structural basis of RNA-guided transcription by a dCas12f–σ<sup>E</sup>–RNAP complex
papers.authors: Renjian Xiao, Florian T. Hoffmann, Dan Xie, Tanner Wiegand, Adriana I. Palmieri et al. (+2 more)
papers.summary: A study of the dCas12f–σE–RNAP complex reveals how it recruits RNAP to targeted DNA, initiating transcription at a fixed downstream distance, bypassing canonical −35 recognition and stabilizing the −10 element.

papers.title: Homologous recombination deficiency and hemizygosity drive resistance in breast cancer
papers.authors: Anton Safonov, Minna Lee, David N. Brown, Luca Boscolo Bielo, Miika Mehine et al. (+52 more)
papers.summary: Germline and somatic interactions define actionable genomic patterns driving acquired therapy resistance in breast cancer, providing insights into breast cancer treatment.

papers.title: Exapted CRISPR–Cas12f homologues drive RNA-guided transcription
papers.authors: Florian T. Hoffmann, Tanner Wiegand, Adriana I. Palmieri, Juniper Glass-Klaiber, Renjian Xiao et al. (+6 more)
papers.summary: Specialized σ factors interact with nuclease-dead, CRISPR–Cas12f proteins to form potent, RNA-guided gene activation systems that function independently of fixed promoter motifs.

papers.title: Wide-swath altimetry maps bank shapes and storage changes in global rivers
papers.authors: A. Cerbelaud, J. Wade, C. H. David, M. Durand, R. P. M. Frasson et al. (+2 more)
papers.summary: A new satellite mission provides observations of river channel geometry and monthly water storage changes for 126,674 river reaches worldwide, offering insights into global river dynamics.

papers.title: Largest Silurian fish illuminates the origin of osteichthyan characters
papers.authors: Jing Lu, Brian Choo, Wenjin Zhao, You-an Zhu, Xindong Cui et al. (+9 more)
papers.summary: New findings from an articulated Silurian fish reveal previously unseen morphological details of a stem osteichthyan, providing insights into the origin of osteichthyan characters.

papers.title: Precancerous niche remodelling dictates nascent tumour persistence
papers.authors: G. Skrupskelyte, J. E. Rojo Arias, H. Ajith, Y. Dang, D. Rossetti et al. (+16 more)
papers.summary: Summary unavailable.

papers.summary: Summary unavailable.

papers.title: Fresh claim of making elusive ‘hexagonal’ diamond is the strongest yet
papers.authors: Mark Peplow
papers.summary: Summary unavailable.

papers.title: Stress-hormone signalling protects spreading cancer cells from immune system
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Summary unavailable.

papers.title: AI can write genomes — how long until it creates synthetic life?
papers.authors: Ewen Callaway
papers.summary: Summary unavailable.

papers.title: Magnetic fluid offers better seal in heart-plugging medical procedure
papers.authors: Yu Shrike Zhang
papers.summary: Researchers have developed an injectable fluid that can be used to close off part of the heart in animals, potentially improving a procedure that prevents stroke in people with irregular heartbeats.

papers.title: Funding from individual donors: lessons from the Epstein case
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Universities need to establish and empower compliance teams to ensure adherence to ethical funding policies, particularly in light of the Epstein case.

papers.title: A genetic switch turns off parental behaviour and drives infanticide in male striped mice
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Research on African striped mice has found that male parenting behavior is influenced by the social environment and expression level of the Agouti gene, with some males exhibiting hostile behavior towards pups.

papers.title: Do wet or dry soils trigger thunderstorms? It depends on how the wind blows
papers.authors: Craig R. Ferguson
papers.summary: Satellite data have shown that wind conditions play a crucial role in the connection between soil moisture and thunderstorms, which could be used to inform forecasting.

papers.title: Climate change and geopolitics threaten water supplies — but disaster is not inevitable
papers.authors: Ali Tauqeer Sheikh
papers.summary: The Indus Waters Treaty has withstood several challenges, including armed conflicts and glacier loss, and could serve as a blueprint for other regions facing water supply threats.

papers.title: Cancer blood tests are everywhere. Do they really work?
papers.authors: Nic Fleming
papers.summary: A review of cancer blood tests raises concerns about their accuracy and potential to miss or misdiagnose cancers.

papers.title: Skin cells boost distant antibody responses
papers.authors: Xueping Zhu, Caroline L. Sokol
papers.summary: Researchers discovered a pathway in skin cells that boosts the production of antibodies to protect the whole body.

papers.title: How a mathematician is cracking open Mexico’s powerful drug cartels
papers.authors: Gemma Conroy
papers.summary: A mathematician developed models to help combat organized crime in Mexico, potentially improving public safety.

papers.title: Under pressure: the reality of Mexico’s research system
papers.authors: Oscar Xavier Guerrero Gutiérrez
papers.summary: Mexican scientists face unstable research conditions, and efforts are needed to support them.

papers.title: Radiology AI makes consistent diagnoses using 3D images from different health centres
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A 3D vision-language model called Merlin demonstrated strong performance in radiology diagnoses across different hospitals.

papers.title: Daily briefing: The return of the snail — the month’s best science images
papers.authors: Flora Graham
papers.summary: This paper is a collection of science images from February, including a snail, cancer blood tests, and a starry night sky.

papers.title: Hunt for reactive metabolites uncovers unusual chemistry in a human pathogen
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Researchers developed a method to capture reactive metabolites from a human pathogen, discovering two unusual molecules with potential synthetic applications.

papers.title: Author Correction: Healthy forests safeguard traditional wild meat food systems in Amazonia
papers.authors: André Pinassi Antunes, Pedro de Araujo Lima Constantino, Julia E. Fa, Daniel P. Munari, Thais Q. Morcatty et al. (+53 more)
papers.summary: This is an author correction for a previous paper on the importance of healthy forests for traditional wild meat food systems in Amazonia.

papers.title: Genetically encoded assembly recorder temporally resolves cellular history
papers.authors: Yuqing Yan, Jiaxi Lu, Zhe Li, Zuohan Zhao, Timothy F. Shay et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: Scientists developed a genetically encoded assembly recorder that can temporally resolve cellular history, providing new insights into cellular processes.

papers.title: Jam-packed star system is most compact of its kind ever found
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Astronomers discovered a compact star system with four Sun-like stars that would fit within Jupiter's orbit, providing new insights into star formation and planetary systems.

papers.title: Releasing open-weight AI in steps would alleviate risks
papers.authors: S. M. Nuruzzaman Nobel, Maxine Tan
papers.summary: Releasing open-weight AI in steps could reduce risks associated with its development and deployment.

papers.title: From the first telephone to videoconferencing in 100 years
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A historical article highlighting the progress of telecommunications over the past 100 years.

papers.title: South Korea’s AI framework act focuses on rights and safety
papers.authors: WooJung Jon
papers.summary: South Korea's AI framework act prioritizes rights and safety in AI development and deployment.

papers.title: Climate research is global — risks and responsibilities should also be distributed
papers.authors: Zhenhua Rui, Lu Lin, Hongfang Lu, Y. Frank Cheng
papers.summary: Climate research should be a global effort, with risks and responsibilities distributed accordingly.

papers.title: Dynamics of natural selection preceding human viral epidemics and pandemics

papers.summary: Viral adaptation in the host reservoir is not a necessary precursor to novel zoonotic virus epidemics and pandemics.

papers.authors: Shan Liu, Puneeth Guruprasad, Ranjani Ramasubramanian, Bhoomi Madhu, Luca Paruzzo, Kecheng Han, Andre Kelly, Alexander Shestov, He N. Xu, Alberto Carturan, Chaoting Zhou, Kevin R. Amses, Amichay Afriat, Lev Litichevskiy, Jason Lin, Ezra Dubowitz, Neil Tangal, Jing Zhang, Alana McSween, Melody Tan, Federico Stella, Andrew Lee, Siena Nason, Xianxin Hua, Michael Schneider, Madeleine Sleeman, Yunlin Zhang, Giulia Gabrielli, Ziqi Yang, Raymone Pajarillo, Ruchi Patel, Guido Ghilardi, Vrutti Patel, Akshita Joshi, Shunzhou Jiang, Yanqing Jiang, Patrizia Porazzi, Julia C. Tchou, Brian Keith, Mingyao Li, Elise Chong, Stephen J. Schuster, Michael Milone, Joshua Rabinowitz, Roddy S. O’Connor, Christoph A. Thaiss, Maayan Levy, Marco Ruella
papers.summary: β-hydroxybutyrate (BHB) enhances the metabolic fitness of CAR T cells in cancer, making them more effective at fighting tumors.

papers.title: Light-directed evolution of dynamic, multi-state, and computational protein functionalities
papers.authors: Vojislav Gligorovski, Marco Labagnara, Lorenzo Scutteri, Marius Blackholm, Andreas Möglich, Nahal Mansouri, Sahand Jamal Rahi
papers.summary: A new method called optovolution uses light to evolve complex protein behaviors, such as logic gates, that were previously difficult to achieve.

papers.title: Selective targeting of endothelial and perivascular angiocrine ROCK2 treats liver fibrosis
papers.authors: Yan Hu, Biao Yang, Chengju Xiao, Xinchun Yang, Hua Zhang, Pengbo Yang, Xiao Du, Guixiang Zhang, Beibei Liang, Nan Bai, Daoguang Zhang, Dongbo Wu, Qinghua Luo, Yangjing Teng, Yumeng Chen, Zhiqing Guo, Chengjian Zhao, Tinghong Ye, Dong Chai, Xiaolong Qi, Weiting Zhong, Jie Chen, Haohao Dong, Jinhang Gao, Huanhuan He, Junlei Chang, Xuri Li, Liang Peng, Shahin Rafii, Scott L. Friedman, Cheng Yi, Yun Cai, Yanping Zhao, Hongjun Wang, Chen Wang, Zhongwei Cao, Bi-Sen Ding
papers.summary: Targeting a specific protein called ROCK2 in endothelial and perivascular cells can treat liver fibrosis by reducing fibrogenic vascular dysfunction.

papers.title: Deltaviruses spread through a viral Trojan Horse
papers.authors: Joe McKellar, Aurélien Fouillen, Sébastien Lyonnais, Marie-Pierre Blanchard, Florian Seigneuret, Antonio Trullo, Zoé Denis, Roni Sleiman, Brandon Le Bon, Laurence Berry, Lorena Kumarasinghe, Jules Bouget, Arinya Apostel, Maria Bacia-Verloop, Sylvain De Rossi, Yasmine Messaoud-Nacer, Sophie Colomb, Solange Desagher, Isabelle K. Vila, Nadine Laguette, Sébastien Granier, Hugues de Rocquigny, Raphael Gaudin, Valérie Courgnaud, Irina Gutsche, Jussi Hepojoki, Karim Majzoub
papers.summary: Some satellite viruses can spread by hiding inside helper virus particles, allowing them to reach new animal species and organs they couldn't reach alone.

papers.title: Metabolite-gated vascular contractility switch: OXGR1 activation mechanism enables agonist therapy for rosacea erythema
papers.authors: Wenqin Xiao, Yan Zhu, Xinjie Tang, Kongkai Zhu, Weifeng Zhang, Mengting Chen, Kui Cai, San Xu, Zheng Wu, Mei Wang, Jiayi Liu, Linglong Long, Zixin Tan, Aike Wu, Songqi Zhou, Zhixiang Zhao, Yan Tang, Yingxue Huang, Ben Wang, Fangfen Liu, Qian Wang, Fan Yang, Dan Jian, Wei Shi, Hongfu Xie, Xiang Chen, Lulu Guo, Zhili Deng, Jinpeng Sun, Ji Li
papers.summary: A new metabolite called α-KG is identified as a key player in rosacea, and a selective agonist called A-1 is developed to alleviate erythema in rosacea-like models.

papers.title: Genetically encoded fluorescent reporters to visualize α-synuclein pathology in live brain
papers.authors: Li Zhang, Minhui Yu, Guoqing Chen, Siyuan Ge, Mengdi Wang, Xianying Zhang, Miao Zhao, Huating Gu, Meizhu Huang, Aixue Liu, Gengxin Ran, Zeyuan Liu, Tiepeng Liao, Qi Chen, Chenjian Miao, Yao Lu, Yibing Wang, Fengchao Wang, Zhihui Liu, Hongying Zhu, Qian Wu, Zhuan Zhou, Wei Xiong, Xiaoqun Wang, Peng Cao
papers.summary: Researchers developed genetically encoded fluorescent reporters to visualize α-synuclein pathology in live brain cells, enabling the study of the propagation and pathological effects of α-synuclein inclusions.

papers.title: Efficient amyloid-β degradation in Alzheimer’s disease using SPYTACs
papers.authors: Fei Teng, Jing Liu, Tongtong Cui, Xiangtian Tan, Kailun Liu, Zongren Hou, Li Zhou, Yuanzhi Xie, Rongqi Li, Da Li, Bojin Li, Dongmei Wang, Qi Zhou, Baoyang Hu, Wei Li
papers.summary: A synthetic peptide-programmed targeted protein degradation platform called SPYTAC efficiently degrades amyloid-β in the brain and periphery, preserving neurons and improving cognition in mice with Alzheimer's disease.

papers.title: Restoring circadian rhythms in the hypothalamic paraventricular nucleus reverses aging biomarkers and extends lifespan in male mice
papers.authors: Haijiao Zhao, Meimei Liao, Ran Huo, Ting He, Hongni Tian, Zeqi Li, Chen Chen, Ziqing Yu, Juan Chai, Xiaocui Song, Ruichao Shao, Shuhua Ying, Wen Gao, Ling Liu, Di Sang, Qi Li, Haohong Li, Fengchao Wang, Dapeng Ju, Eric Erquan Zhang
papers.summary: Enhancing circadian rhythms in the hypothalamic paraventricular nucleus neurons of mice using 3′-deoxyadenosine treatment alleviates age-related pathologies and extends lifespan.

papers.title: Mettl3 promotes reprogramming and axonogenesis of induced retinal ganglion cells
papers.authors: Ke Zhang, Huilin Liang, Shuyi Chen
papers.summary: Researchers discovered that Mettl3 promotes reprogramming and axonogenesis of induced retinal ganglion cells by driving m6A modification and accelerating modified transcript degradation.

papers.title: Genome-edited safe and immune-evasive human pluripotent cells: Potential solution for allogeneic therapies
papers.authors: Vivian Tam, Nicole Ching Man Wong, Andrew Chung Hin Poon, Mengxia Zhu, Ting Mei, Janette Kwok, Patrick Chu, Eric D. Jong, Jean Kit Tang, Andras Nagy, Danny Chan
papers.summary: Scientists created immune-evasive human pluripotent cells using combined SafeCell and AlloAccept engineered technologies, which can be used for allogeneic therapies without the need for immunosuppression.

papers.title: Delayed forebrain excitatory and inhibitory neurogenesis in STRADA-related megalencephaly via mTOR hyperactivity
papers.authors: Tong Pan, Grace Lin, Xuan Li, Debora VanHeyningen, John C. Walker, Sahej Kohli, Aiswarya Saravanan, Amrita Kondur, Daniel C. Jaklic, Saul Pantoja-Gutierrez, Shivanshi Vaid, Julie Sturza, Ken Inoki, Tomozumi Imamichi, Weizhong Chang, Louis T. Dang
papers.summary: STRADA loss leads to mTOR hyperactivity, increasing neural stem cell renewal and delaying neurogenesis in human forebrain organoids. This is associated with altered interneuron development and metabolic pathways. mTOR inhibition rescues most changes, suggesting a potential therapeutic strategy for STRADA-related disease.

papers.title: Establishing mouse forebrain organoids as models of intrinsic cortical network assembly
papers.authors: Sebastian Hernandez, Hunter E. Schweiger, Isabel Cline, Gregory A. Kaurala, Ash Robbins, Daniel Solis, Samira Vera-Choqqueccota, Jinghui Geng, Tjitse van der Molen, Francisco Reyes, Chinweike Norman Asogwa, Kateryna Voitiuk, Mattia Chini, Marco Rolandi, Sofie R. Salama, Bradley M. Colquitt, Tal Sharf, David Haussler, Mircea Teodorescu, Mohammed A. Mostajo-Radji
papers.summary: Mouse forebrain organoids self-organize into small-world neural networks, with dorsal organoids showing progressive synchronization and ventral organoids developing enhanced clustering and modularity. This reveals how cellular composition shapes cortical network topology.

papers.title: Genomic characterization of sub-populations in human pluripotent stem cell-derived retinal progenitor cells driving retinal lamination
papers.authors: Yasuaki Iwama, Tomohiro Masuda, Mika Yoshimura, Mikiya Watanabe, Yoko Ohigashi, Martin Friedlander, Kohji Nishida, Masayo Takahashi, Itoshi Nikaido, Michiko Mandai
papers.summary: Prolonged proliferation with sequential WNT signaling is associated with retinal laminar structures in human embryonic stem cell-derived retinal spheroids. Despite distinct in vitro architectures, both well-layered and non-layered spheroids generate aligned photoreceptors with potential functional integration after transplantation.

papers.title: Selective Cav1.3 inhibition promotes survival of transplanted dopaminergic neurons via the CaMKII-p65-p53 pathway
papers.authors: Qingyuan Y. Tang, Jing Sun, Jun Xue, Chen Qiu, Tian Wang, Tatyana Matveeva, Yifan F. Wu, Xiaoying Y. Li, Tianlin L. Cheng, Hyemyung Seo, Mingtao T. Li, Shanshan S. Ma, Jeffrey S. Schweitzer, Bin Song
papers.summary: Inhibiting the calcium channel Cav1.3 in stem cell-derived dopamine neurons protects them from inflammatory and toxic damage by blocking the CaMKII-p65-p53 pathway. This strategy significantly improves transplanted mDAN survival, offering a promising approach to enhance cell therapy for Parkinson’s disease.

papers.title: Messenger RNA delivery into Sertoli cells restores fertility to congenitally infertile male mice
papers.authors: Mito Kanatsu-Shinohara, Narumi Ogonuki, Shogo Matoba, Takehiro Miyazaki, Hiroko Morimoto, Tianjiao Liu, Naoto Yoshinaga, Atsuo Ogura, Satoshi Uchida, Takashi Shinohara
papers.summary: A method to deliver mRNA into Sertoli cells with minimal innate immune activation was developed. Delivery of wild-type Cldn11 mRNA restored haploid round spermatids in congenitally infertile Cldn11-deficient mice, demonstrating the therapeutic potential of mRNA-based infertility treatment.

papers.title: CD133/CD49a discriminate between human pluripotent stem cell-derived pancreatic beta and alpha cells
papers.authors: Chenglei Tian, Yilin Di, Aisha Muhammad, Henrik Semb
papers.summary: Researchers identified CD133 as a surface marker that can distinguish between pancreatic beta and alpha cells derived from human pluripotent stem cells.

papers.title: Re-evaluating the site-directed nuclease classification as a regulatory trigger for genome-edited plant products
papers.authors: Osman Mewett, John McMurdy, Lieselot Bertho, Ana Atanassova, Naomi Stevens et al. (+4 more)
papers.summary: The current site-directed nuclease classification system for regulating genome-edited plant products is inadequate and should be replaced with a science-based, outcome-focused approach.

papers.title: Quantification and transcriptome profiling reveal abundant, dynamic and translatable dephospho-CoA-capped RNAs
papers.authors: Hao Hu, Qiyue Zhang, Xuan Ma, Hsu-Feng Chu, Han Wang et al. (+10 more)
papers.summary: Researchers developed a method to quantify and analyze dephospho-CoA-capped RNAs in cells using an enzyme that specifically recognizes these RNAs.

papers.title: Sharing big data for sustainable agri-food innovation
papers.authors: Erik Alexandersson, Thies Marten Heick, Anna Chailyan, Sisse Marquina-Jongberg, Thomas de Bang et al. (+1 more)
papers.summary: The article discusses the importance of sharing big data for sustainable agri-food innovation, but no specific findings or methods are presented.

papers.title: Large-scale library of 3D ant images captured with synchrotron X-ray microtomography
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Researchers created a large-scale library of 3D images of ants using synchrotron X-ray microtomography, providing a valuable resource for studying ant diversity.

papers.title: High-throughput phenomics of global ant biodiversity
papers.authors: Julian Katzke, Francisco Hita Garcia, Philipp D. Lösel, Fumika Azuma, Tomáš Faragó et al. (+73 more)
papers.summary: A database called Antscan has been created to store X-ray CT images of ants from almost 800 species, which will be coordinated with genome sequencing projects for integrative analyses.

papers.title: It’s a colorful pangenome world
papers.authors: Vivien Marx
papers.summary: Pangenomes can reveal the spectrum of genome variation within a species, and tools for working with them are becoming more available.

papers.title: Magnon confinement in epitaxial antiferromagnetic oxide heterostructures
papers.authors: Sajid Husain, Maya Ramesh, Xinyan Li, Sergei Prokhorenko, Shashank Kumar Ojha et al. (+19 more)
papers.summary: Researchers have found a way to confine magnon transport to a plane by sandwiching BiFeO3 between layers of non-polar antiferromagnetic LaFeO3, which could be used for electric-field-controlled spin-based memory and logic elements.

papers.title: Heterogeneity by design
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Heterogeneous microstructure engineering has become a powerful strategy for optimizing properties and performance of metallic alloys.

papers.title: Dynamical stability by spin transfer in nearly isotropic magnets
papers.authors: Hidekazu Kurebayashi, Joseph Barker, Takumi Yamazaki, Varun K. Kushwaha, Kilian D. Stenning et al. (+7 more)
papers.summary: Researchers have found that spin torques generated via the spin-Hall effect in CoFeB/W/MgO can stabilize magnetization in a high-energy anti-parallel state relative to an applied magnetic field, which holds promise for realizing unconventional computing paradigms.

papers.title: In situ-generated vaccine-like pyroptosome for personalized cancer immunotherapy
papers.authors: Binlong Chen, Fangjie Wan, Heming Xia, Xingquan Pan, Letong Wang et al. (+14 more)
papers.summary: Researchers developed a new injectable nanoadjuvant that induces pyroptosis in tumors, leading to a strong vaccine-like response that can eradicate cancer.

papers.title: Texture-dependent all-optical switching in ferromagnetic films via stochastic nucleation of nanoscale domains
papers.authors: Dinar Khusyainov, Rein Liefferink, MengXing Na, Fabian Kammerbauer, Robert Frömter et al. (+8 more)
papers.summary: Scientists discovered a new method for all-optical switching in ferromagnetic films using circularly polarized picosecond optical pulses.
